attacking_style: Launches repeated accelerations in final 40km, with decisive moves typically 500m-4km from finish on steep gradients or gravel sectors. Prefers explosive attacks at steepest sections rather than sustained climbing efforts.
team_tactics: Minimal domestique support; often functions as own lead-out or even leads out teammates despite protected status. Alpecin shows coordination issues in sprint finales, forcing self-reliance in positioning and timing.
terrain_preference: Excels on punchy climbs (500m steep gradients), gravel sectors, and technical finales requiring power-endurance. Exposed on sustained Cat-1 climbs beyond 10km and stage race mountain scenarios where one-day power fades.
vulnerability: Premature aggressive attacks cost him in late-race execution (cramping at 50m after 39km solo effort). Stage race fatigue evident when attempting to sustain Monument-level efforts beyond single days. Early crashes disrupt rhythm significantly.
key_patterns: Attacks multiple times before decisive move; rarely wins from single effort (6+ races)
Exceptional recovery after being dropped; regains contact in unlikely scenarios (Amstel, Flanders)
Monument specialist with 2023 doubling capability; struggles translating to GT climbing beyond Week 1
Prioritizes team leadout duties over personal glory even in green jersey contention
Timing attacks to steepest 500m gradient sections rather than climb bases (4+ races)
Cramping/emptying when attacking too far out (39km Flanders, 205km Tirreno solo)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Strade Bianche abandonment showed concerning form drop from 2021 win; mechanical at Flanders cost Monument double despite Roubaix redemption
built_from: 2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
